attractions.name: Wrangell-St. Elias National Park & Preserve
attractions.season: Both gravel access roads usable late May-Sept; unmaintained in winter
attractions.blurb: Largest US national park at 13.2 million acres; 9 of the 16 highest US peaks and four ranges collide here.
attractions.tip: No entrance fee and no loop road. Pick one arm: McCarthy Road to Kennecott, or Nabesna Road for solitude.

attractions.name: Wrangell-St. Elias Visitor Center (Copper Center)
attractions.season: Daily late May-early Sept; reduced weekday hours off-season
attractions.blurb: Main NPS center at Richardson Hwy Mile 106.8: park film, relief map, Ahtna exhibits, big deck view of the volcanoes.
attractions.tip: Only staffed center on the Richardson - get McCarthy/Nabesna road conditions and loaner bear cans here.
attractions.duration_note: quick stop en route to Chitina or Valdez

attractions.name: Kennecott Mines National Historic Landmark
attractions.season: Tours and services mid-May to mid-Sept; buildings stand year-round
attractions.blurb: Red 14-story copper concentration mill and 1900s company town, abandoned in 1938, now NPS-stabilized.
attractions.tip: Mill interior is guided-tour-only via St. Elias Alpine Guides (~2hr); walking the town exterior is free.
attractions.duration_note: full day including shuttle from the footbridge

attractions.name: Root Glacier Trail
attractions.season: Snow-free June-Sept; guided ice walks mid-May to mid-Sept
attractions.blurb: 2mi mostly-flat trail from the Kennecott mill to walkable white ice - the park's signature day hike.
attractions.tip: Rent microspikes in Kennecott. Unguided walking is legal but stay off the crevassed upper ice.

attractions.name: McCarthy
attractions.season: Businesses open late May-mid Sept; near-empty the rest of the year
attractions.blurb: End-of-road settlement of ~30 year-rounders 5mi below Kennecott; saloon, lodges, shuttles, reached on foot.
attractions.tip: Park at the Kennicott River footbridge (Mile 59.4) and walk or shuttle in - outside vehicles can't cross.

attractions.name: Bonanza Mine Trail
attractions.season: Melts out late June; snow returns September
attractions.blurb: 4.5mi, 3,800ft climb from Kennecott to the Bonanza mine ruins at 6,000ft, with Chugach-to-Wrangells views.
attractions.tip: 8-9hr round trip. Start by 8am and carry all your water - there is none above the mill town.

attractions.name: McCarthy Road
attractions.season: Drivable roughly late May-Sept; not maintained for winter travel
attractions.blurb: 60mi gravel road on the old Copper River & Northwestern railbed, Chitina to the Kennicott River footbridge.
attractions.tip: Allow 2hr each way and carry a full-size spare - old rail spikes still surface. Most rental contracts ban it.

attractions.name: Kuskulana Bridge
attractions.season: Reachable whenever McCarthy Road is open, late May-Sept
attractions.blurb: 1910 steel railroad bridge at McCarthy Road Mile 17: 525ft long, 238ft above the Kuskulana River gorge.
attractions.tip: Park at the far pullout and walk back across - the deck is one lane with a plank walkway on the side.

attractions.name: Gilahina Trestle
attractions.season: Reachable whenever McCarthy Road is open, late May-Sept
attractions.blurb: Collapsing 1911 wooden rail trestle, 880ft long and ~90ft high, built in eight days; McCarthy Road Mile 29.
attractions.tip: Roadside pullout with a short spur path. The timbers are rotten - photograph from below, don't climb.

attractions.name: McCarthy Airport flightseeing
attractions.season: Flights mid-May to mid-Sept, weather permitting
attractions.blurb: Gravel strip beside McCarthy; bush flights over the Root and Kennicott glaciers, Bagley Icefield and Mt Blackburn.
attractions.tip: Flights run 35-90 min. The same operator flies Chitina-McCarthy if you would rather skip the gravel road.
attractions.duration_note: book a clear morning, keep a backup day

attractions.name: Mount Blackburn
attractions.season: Visible year-round; clearest on cold bright days
attractions.blurb: 16,390ft ice-capped extinct shield volcano, Alaska's fifth-highest peak and the white wall above Kennecott.
attractions.tip: Best ground view is from the Kennecott mill town lawn or the Root Glacier trail on a clear morning.

attractions.name: Mount Wrangell
attractions.season: Visible year-round from the Richardson Highway
attractions.blurb: 14,163ft active shield volcano that still steams from summit fumaroles; anchors the Copper Basin skyline.
attractions.tip: Best roadside look is Willow Lake overlook or the visitor center deck at Copper Center; steam shows in cold air.

attractions.name: Nabesna Road
attractions.season: Best June-Sept; creek fords run high in spring melt and after heavy rain
attractions.blurb: 42mi gravel spur from Slana into the park's quiet north side: trailheads, tundra, and almost no traffic.
attractions.tip: 2WD is fine to Mile 28; the Trail, Lost and Boyden creek fords past there need clearance. No fuel on the road.

attractions.name: Slana Ranger Station
attractions.season: Staffed late May-early Sept
attractions.blurb: Small NPS station at Nabesna Road Mile 0.2 - road and ford conditions, permits, bear cans, trail beta.
attractions.tip: Stop or call before driving Nabesna Road; the creek crossings change week to week all summer.

attractions.name: Skookum Volcano Trail
attractions.season: Snow-free late June-Sept
attractions.blurb: 2.5mi one-way route up an eroded volcanic vent at Nabesna Road Mile 36: lava dikes and an alpine pass.
attractions.tip: Route follows a creek bed and is cairned, not cut. Scan the upper slopes for Dall sheep from the pass.

attractions.name: Chitina
attractions.season: Services thin but present May-Sept; very quiet in winter
attractions.blurb: Old railway town at the McCarthy Road junction: last fuel, a small store, NPS ranger station, dipnet hub.
attractions.tip: Fill the tank here - there is no fuel on the 120mi round trip to McCarthy and back.

attractions.name: Copper River fish wheels at Chitina
attractions.season: Wheels turn roughly mid-June through August
attractions.blurb: Ahtna subsistence and personal-use fish wheels scoop salmon from the silty Copper below the Chitina bridge.
attractions.tip: View from the bridge pullout on McCarthy Road Mile 1. The wheels are private property - watch, don't touch.

attractions.name: Chitina dipnet fishery (O'Brien Creek)
attractions.season: Opens ~June 10 by emergency order, peaks late June-July, closes Sept 30
attractions.blurb: Alaska's biggest personal-use dipnet fishery - people rope onto cliffs above the Copper and net sockeye.
attractions.tip: Alaska residents only. O'Brien Creek Rd is rough and the canyon trail beyond it is genuinely dangerous.
attractions.duration_note: full day; visitors can only watch

attractions.name: Liberty Falls State Recreation Site
attractions.season: Open May-Sept; gate closed in winter
attractions.blurb: Roadside waterfall and 10-site campground at Edgerton Hwy Mile 23.5; steep path climbs to the upper cascade.
attractions.tip: Best free leg-stretch on the way to McCarthy - the lower falls are 100 yards from the parking area.

attractions.name: Edgerton Highway
attractions.season: Paved and open year-round
attractions.blurb: 33mi paved spur off the Richardson at Kenny Lake down to Chitina, with the Wrangell volcanoes on the horizon.
attractions.tip: Paved the whole way - the gravel only starts past Chitina. Last groceries are at Kenny Lake Mercantile.

attractions.name: Willow Lake Overlook
attractions.season: Open year-round; lake ices over Nov-Apr
attractions.blurb: Richardson Hwy pullout where the whole Wrangell volcano wall - Drum, Sanford, Wrangell, Blackburn - reflects in the lake.
attractions.tip: Shoot early morning for glass-calm water; interpretive signs name every peak on the skyline.

attractions.name: Klutina River
attractions.season: King salmon mid-June to mid-July; sockeye July-August
attractions.blurb: Glacial Copper tributary at Copper Center known for big king salmon in June and heavy sockeye runs in July.
attractions.tip: Bank access is mostly private land - pay a day fee at a Copper Center lodge or book a drift boat.
attractions.duration_note: full day; guided trips fill early

attractions.name: Glennallen
attractions.season: Year-round services
attractions.blurb: Highway-junction service town: fuel, groceries, clinic, and the last real supermarket before McCarthy.
attractions.tip: Richardson x Glenn junction sits at Richardson Mile 187. Gas is pricey here but options thin out fast.

attractions.name: Richardson Highway (Valdez-Glennallen)
attractions.season: Open year-round; Thompson Pass closes for storms in winter
attractions.blurb: Alaska's first road, Valdez to Fairbanks; the southern half climbs Thompson Pass past glaciers and waterfalls.
attractions.tip: Valdez-Glennallen is 115mi / 2.5hr. Drive it southbound for the big reveal dropping into Keystone Canyon.

attractions.name: Worthington Glacier State Recreation Site
attractions.season: Parking plowed most of the year; trail snow-free June-Sept
attractions.blurb: Drive-up glacier at Richardson Mile 28.7 with a paved path to a viewing shelter and a rough moraine trail to the ice.
attractions.tip: Easiest roadside glacier in Alaska after Mendenhall. Don't walk onto the ice unguided - it is crevassed.

attractions.name: Thompson Pass
attractions.season: Open year-round but closes for storms; wildflowers July-Aug
attractions.blurb: 2,678ft treeless Chugach gap on the Richardson - the snowiest highway pass in North America.
attractions.tip: Holds the US 24hr snow record (62in, 1955) and 974in in one season. Fog is constant; stop when it lifts.

attractions.name: Blueberry Lake State Recreation Site
attractions.season: Open roughly June-Sept; snowbound the rest of the year
attractions.blurb: Alpine lake and 25-site campground just below Thompson Pass, ringed by rock walls; stocked with trout.
attractions.tip: Best tent camping on the Richardson but fully exposed and cold. Sites fill by late afternoon in July.

attractions.name: Thompson Pass heli-skiing (Tsaina Lodge)
attractions.season: Heli season roughly March-April; lodge closed much of summer
attractions.blurb: Richardson Mile 34.7 lodge that anchors Chugach heli-skiing on 5,000ft spine walls above Thompson Pass.
attractions.tip: Buy multi-day packages - down days for weather are normal. This is expert terrain, not a beginner trip.

attractions.name: Keystone Canyon
attractions.season: Open year-round; waterfalls biggest late May-June
attractions.blurb: Narrow Lowe River gorge at Richardson Mile 13-17, walls hung with waterfalls; also a Class III raft run.
attractions.tip: Falls run fullest at snowmelt. The hand-cut 1905 railroad tunnel is a signed pullout near Mile 14.9.

attractions.name: Bridal Veil & Horsetail Falls
attractions.season: Peak flow late May-June; frozen ice-climbing routes Dec-Mar
attractions.blurb: Two roadside waterfalls a quarter mile apart in Keystone Canyon; Bridal Veil drops ~900ft off the rim.
attractions.tip: Pullouts are on the canyon side and spray reaches the road. In winter both become classic ice climbs.

attractions.name: Valdez
attractions.season: Year-round; tour season June-Aug, heli-ski season Mar-Apr
attractions.blurb: Fjord-head port rebuilt after the 1964 quake: pipeline terminus, fish derbies, glacier cruises, 300in of snow.
attractions.tip: 6hr drive from Anchorage or a 6hr ferry from Whittier - the ferry saves backtracking up the Glenn Highway.
attractions.duration_note: 2 nights to do the cruise plus a hike

attractions.name: Columbia Glacier cruise
attractions.season: Daily sailings roughly mid-May to mid-Sept
attractions.blurb: Half-day boat from Valdez through miles of icebergs to a huge tidewater glacier front; otters, sea lions, kittiwakes.
attractions.tip: Two operators run it - Stan Stephens and Lu-Lu Belle. The glacier has retreated far, so boats stop at the ice field.

attractions.name: Valdez Marine Terminal
attractions.season: Viewable year-round from Dayville Road
attractions.blurb: South end of the 800mi Trans-Alaska Pipeline: 18 storage tanks and tanker berths across the bay from town.
attractions.tip: Closed to the public since 2001 - view it from Dayville Road pullouts or from a Columbia Glacier boat.

attractions.name: Old Valdez townsite
attractions.season: Accessible year-round; interpretive signs best May-Sept
attractions.blurb: Empty grid of streets and foundations 4mi east of town, abandoned after the 1964 quake destroyed the waterfront.
attractions.tip: Memorial plaques list the dead from Valdez and Chenega; signs mark where the docks and hotels stood.

attractions.name: Valdez Museum & Historical Archive
attractions.season: Daily 9-5 through September; reduced winter hours
attractions.blurb: Town museum on Egan Drive covering the 1964 quake, pipeline construction, the Exxon Valdez spill and the gold-rush trail.
attractions.tip: One ticket also covers the Old Town museum on Hazelet Ave and its detailed scale model of pre-quake Valdez.

attractions.name: Solomon Gulch Hatchery
attractions.season: Pink salmon return late June-early Aug; coho July-Oct
attractions.blurb: North America's largest pink-salmon hatchery on Dayville Road; in July the creek is solid fish and bears.
attractions.tip: Free viewing platform. Black bears, sea lions and gulls work the outfall - stay near the car and give way.

attractions.name: Allison Point
attractions.season: Pinks late June-Aug; silvers Aug-Sept
attractions.blurb: Dayville Road shoreline where hatchery salmon stack up - shore fishing, a gravel campground, and constant bear traffic.
attractions.tip: You can catch salmon from the beach with no boat; combine it with the hatchery a mile further along.

attractions.name: Valdez Glacier Lake
attractions.season: Ice-free paddling roughly mid-May to Sept
attractions.blurb: Iceberg-choked meltwater lake 5mi from town - the local kayak and packraft trip among calved bergs and ice caves.
attractions.tip: Guided paddles run mid-May-Sept. Bergs roll without warning and the water is near freezing; go with a guide.

attractions.name: Mineral Creek Trail
attractions.season: Road and trail usable June-Sept
attractions.blurb: Rough gravel road behind Valdez turning to trail up a waterfall canyon to the 1913 Smith stamp mill ruins.
attractions.tip: Drive ~5mi of narrow road, then walk the last mile. High clearance helps; bears use the canyon constantly.

attractions.name: Dock Point Trail
attractions.season: Snow-free May-Oct
attractions.blurb: 1mi gravel and boardwalk loop over a rocky knob at the small boat harbor - the fastest Valdez viewpoint on foot.
attractions.tip: 30-40 minutes, easy with kids, loaded with blueberries in August. Good option in drizzle.

attractions.name: Valdez Ferry Terminal (AMHS)
attractions.season: Sailings year-round but sparse; most frequent May-Sept
attractions.blurb: Alaska Marine Highway dock for the Prince William Sound run to Whittier (~6hr) and on to Cordova.
attractions.tip: Only a few sailings a week and vehicle deck space sells out - reserve months ahead, not on arrival.

attractions.name: Valdez Pioneer Field
attractions.season: Year-round; weather cancellations common
attractions.blurb: Valdez's airstrip 3mi east of town: commuter flights from Anchorage plus the heli-ski and flightseeing base.
attractions.tip: Scheduled service is thin and weather-cancelled often; driving or the ferry is the reliable way in.

attractions.name: Sheep Mountain
attractions.season: Visible year-round; sheep lower on the slopes in spring
attractions.blurb: Banded red-and-buff ridge at Glenn Hwy Mile 113 with a resident Dall sheep band on the slopes above the lodge.
attractions.tip: Scan the scree with binoculars from the lodge pullout - the sheep look like white specks high on the rock.

attractions.name: Matanuska Glacier
attractions.season: Guided ice walks run year-round; best footing May-Sept
attractions.blurb: 27mi valley glacier whose toe nearly reaches the Glenn Highway - Alaska's easiest walk-on ice.
attractions.tip: Access is guided-only across private land now; the gate fee is separate from the tour. Book ahead in July.

attractions.name: Matanuska Glacier State Recreation Site
attractions.season: Open May-Sept; day-use lot plowed inconsistently in winter
attractions.blurb: Free state overlook at Glenn Hwy Mile 101 with a short loop trail and a full-length view down the glacier.
attractions.tip: The no-cost way to see Matanuska Glacier if you skip the paid ice walk; also a quiet 12-site campground.

attractions.name: Glenn Highway National Scenic Byway
attractions.season: Open year-round; winter driving over Eureka can be severe
attractions.blurb: 135mi Palmer-to-Glennallen byway past Matanuska Glacier, Sheep Mountain and 3,322ft Eureka Summit.
attractions.tip: Allow 4hr with stops - this is the standard Anchorage approach to the Wrangells. Fuel at Glacier View and Eureka.

attractions.name: Eureka Summit
attractions.season: Open year-round; ground blizzards common Nov-Mar
attractions.blurb: 3,322ft high point of the Glenn Highway; treeless pass looking at the Nelchina Glacier and the Chugach.
attractions.tip: Eureka Roadhouse is the only service for 40mi. Nelchina caribou sometimes cross the highway in fall.

attractions.name: Lake Louise State Recreation Area
attractions.season: Open water June-Sept; snowmachine and ice fishing Jan-Mar
attractions.blurb: Big shallow lake off the Glenn with lake trout and burbot fishing, a few lodges, and winter snowmachining.
attractions.tip: 19mi gravel spur from Glenn Mile 159.8 - worth the detour only if you're fishing or staying overnight.

attractions.name: Valdez Fish Derbies
attractions.season: Halibut derby late May-Aug; silver derby late July-early Sept
attractions.blurb: Summer-long halibut and silver salmon derbies out of the Valdez small boat harbor, with five-figure cash prizes.
attractions.tip: Buy the derby ticket BEFORE you leave the dock - a fish caught without one doesn't count for prizes.
